wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the HabibiStay platform. Explain its purpose as a short-term rental marketplace tailored for Riyadh, emphasizing the three core value propositions: booking stays, property ownership income, and real estate investment opportunities. Document the full-stack architecture with React frontend and Hono-based backend on Cloudflare Workers. Illustrate the high-level data flow between user interfaces, API endpoints, and database. Include system context diagrams showing external integrations with MyFatoorah, OpenAI, and Google OAuth. Provide a conceptual overview accessible to new developers while detailing technical boundaries and responsibilities across components. Highlight key user journeys for guests, owners, investors, and admins.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the React-based frontend architecture of HabibiStay. Explain the Vite-powered build setup, TypeScript integration, and component-based UI design using functional components and hooks. Detail the role of App.tsx as the root component managing routing and context providers. Describe the styling strategy leveraging Tailwind CSS with utility-first classes and custom configurations in tailwind.config.js. Document the use of React Router for navigation between key pages such as Home, Stays, Dashboard, and AdminDashboard. Include information on asset handling via Vite and global styles defined in index.css. Provide practical examples of component composition patterns used across the application, and illustrate how data flows from API responses to UI rendering. Address performance considerations like code splitting and hydration timing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the React component architecture. Explain the design principles behind reusable UI components such as PropertyCard, Navbar, Footer, and ChatBot. Document component composition patterns, props interfaces, and usage contexts across pages. Highlight how components are structured for maintainability and scalability, including conditional rendering, dynamic data binding, and integration with shared types. Provide code examples showing how PropertyCard is used in Stays and Home pages, and how Navbar dynamically renders based on authentication state. Address accessibility considerations, responsive design implementation via Tailwind, and interaction with global state through context.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create RESTful API documentation for all endpoints defined in the Hono backend. Document each route's HTTP method, URL pattern, request parameters (path, query, body), and response schema. Specify authentication requirements (e.g., JWT, Google OAuth), required headers, and access permissions for each endpoint. Include detailed examples of request and response payloads in JSON format for property listings, bookings, payments, chat interactions, and admin operations. Document status codes, error responses (e.g., 400, 401, 404, 500), and error handling strategies. Cover pagination, filtering, and sorting mechanisms where applicable. Provide sample curl commands for key endpoints such as creating a booking, processing payment, or fetching user dashboard data. Include data validation rules using Zod, field constraints, and required vs optional parameters. Flag any deprecated routes and suggest migration paths. Ensure alignment with frontend API calls in pages like PropertyDetail.tsx and BookingModal.tsx.

wiki_catalogs.name: Properties API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create RESTful API documentation for all property-related endpoints. Document the GET /properties (with filtering by location, dates, price), GET /properties/:id, POST /properties (admin/owner only), PUT /properties/:id, and DELETE /properties/:id routes. Include request parameters such as query filters (location, check-in/check-out dates, guests), path parameters, and request body structure using Property type from shared types. Detail response schemas for single and paginated property responses, including embedded availability and review data. Specify authentication requirements (JWT with role-based access), required headers, and Zod validation rules for input. Provide curl examples for searching properties in Riyadh with date filters and creating a new property listing. Document error responses for invalid queries, unauthorized access, and validation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Property Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Property entity. Detail all fields including id, user_id, title, description, location, price_per_night, capacity, amenities, images, status, and timestamps. Explain the relationship with the User (owner), Bookings, Reviews, and Wishlist. Document foreign key constraints, indexing on user_id and location for query performance, and status transitions (draft, published, disabled). Map the database schema to the Property interface in types.ts and describe how Zod validators in the worker enforce input validation during property creation and updates. Include sample records and describe soft-delete patterns if applicable.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Property Management feature. Explain the implementation of property creation, editing, and listing functionalities in the PropertyForm and PropertyDetail components. Include examples of form handling, image upload integration, and real-time preview. Detail the relationship between property data and other features like booking availability and analytics. Address common issues such as form validation errors, image upload failures, and permission handling. Provide code snippets from both frontend and backend to illustrate data flow and state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Property entity in HabibiStay. wiki_catalogs.name: Booking Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the booking validation sub-feature within the business logic layer. Thoroughly explain implementation details such as date conflict checking, guest count validation, property availability windows, and overlapping reservation prevention. Document how the backend validates booking requests against existing database records using D1 queries, including time-range overlap logic and edge cases like same-day check-in/check-out. Include domain models for BookingRequest and AvailabilityCheck, and explain their integration with Zod for request schema validation. Provide concrete examples from the codebase showing how a POST request to /api/bookings triggers validation logic before proceeding to payment. Address common issues such as race conditions during high concurrency, timezone mismatches in date parsing, and invalid date ranges, along with resolution strategies like transaction locking and input sanitization. Make content accessible to beginners while providing technical depth on query optimization, indexing strategies for date ranges, and use of UTC normalization for consistent time handling.

Include examples of request/response payloads in JSON format, error responses for invalid dates, and performance considerations for indexing date columns. Include examples of retrieving user profile with curl and updating notification preferences. Specify error responses for expired tokens, invalid Google tokens, and forbidden operations. Address GDPR compliance for data access and deletion requests.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Review entity. Specify fields including id, user_id, property_id, rating (1-5), comment, is_verified (to indicate post-stay verification), and created_at. Explain relationships with User and Property tables, and constraints preventing multiple reviews per user per property. Document business rules such as review eligibility only after checkout and moderation workflows. Map to Review interface in types.ts and describe how ratings are aggregated for property averages. Include indexing strategy on property_id and composite indexes for filtering verified reviews. Provide examples of review submission flow and display logic in PropertyDetail.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the User Management feature. Explain the Google OAuth authentication flow, including the AuthCallback handler and JWT token management. Document user profile creation, preferences storage, and session handling. Describe the integration between frontend authentication state and backend identity verification. Include examples of protected routes, role-based access control, and user data synchronization. Address common issues such as token expiration, account linking, and profile updates. Provide guidance on extending authentication to support additional providers and implementing multi-factor authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for this section focused on how the frontend integrates with the backend API in HabibiStay. Explain the data fetching patterns using fetch or a custom hook for communicating with Hono-powered endpoints in src/worker/index.ts. Document the use of shared TypeScript types from types.ts to ensure type safety between frontend and backend. Illustrate common patterns for handling CRUD operations: retrieving property details, creating bookings, managing wishlist items, and fetching user-specific data in Dashboard. Include examples of error handling, loading states, and optimistic UI updates. Address authentication headers, JWT token management, and response normalization strategies. Provide guidance on debugging API calls and mocking responses during development.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for user authentication and profile management endpoints. Document GET /api/auth/google (initiate Google OAuth flow), GET /api/auth/callback (handle OAuth callback, create or update user, issue JWT), GET /api/auth/me (retrieve current user profile), and PUT /api/auth/me (update user preferences). Specify the OAuth 2.0 flow, redirect URIs, and JWT token issuance (stored in HttpOnly cookie). Detail response schema for user object including id, name, email, role (guest, owner, admin), and profile picture. Include security headers (SameSite, Secure) for production. Provide example of JWT payload structure. Document role-based access implications across the API. Include curl examples for testing auth callback (with mock state) and fetching user profile. Explain integration with frontend AuthCallback page and Profile page updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for the Review entity in HabibiStay. Specify all fields: id, user_id, property_id, rating (1-5), comment, is_verified, and timestamps. Document the primary key (id), foreign keys (user_id to User, property_id to Property), and constraints such as rating range validation and unique user-property review constraint. Explain the one-to-many relationships with User and Property. Include a schema diagram from migration files and the TypeScript interface. Provide a sample review record with realistic content. Describe data access patterns in the worker, such as retrieving reviews for a property or calculating average ratings. Discuss performance considerations like indexing on property_id and is_verified for filtering. Address business logic such as ensuring only guests who completed a stay can leave reviews, and moderation workflows for flagged content. Explain how reviews contribute to PropertyAnalytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: External Service Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the external service integration layer that connects HabibiStay with MyFatoorah and OpenAI. Thoroughly explain implementation details of HTTP client configurations, request/response handling, and error resilience patterns. Document how the backend uses fetch or Hono's built-in utilities to communicate securely with third-party APIs, including headers, authentication tokens, and payload formatting. For MyFatoorah, detail payment creation and webhook verification flows; for OpenAI, explain how chat queries are sent to GPT-4o-mini and responses streamed back via the /api/chat endpoint. Include domain models for ServiceRequest, APIResponse, and RetryPolicy. Provide concrete examples from the codebase showing how environment variables (e.g., MYFATOORAH_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY) are injected and how timeouts are handled. Address common issues such as service outages, rate limiting, or authentication failures, and their solutions using exponential backoff, circuit breakers, and fallback responses. Cover configuration options like API base URLs, retry counts, and debug logging levels. Make content accessible to beginners while providing technical depth on observability, monitoring external dependencies, and designing for partial system failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Template Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Email Template Management feature in the Admin Dashboard. Explain how administrators can view, edit, and customize transactional email templates for bookings, payments, and user notifications. Document the integration with the shared email service and template rendering logic. Describe the backend API endpoints that handle template retrieval and updates, including validation and versioning considerations. Include examples of dynamic content insertion (e.g., user names, booking details) and preview functionality. Address security aspects such as preventing XSS in templates and ensuring compliance with email deliverability standards. Provide extension guidance for adding new email types or integrating with third-party email marketing platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: ChatBot Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the ChatBot component, an AI-powered assistant (Sara) that provides real-time guest support using OpenAI's GPT-4o-mini model. Explain its implementation as a floating UI widget with message history, input field, and voice interaction capabilities via useVoiceInterface hook. Document its integration with ChatContext for global state management of messages, loading states, and conversation history. Detail the API communication flow with the /api/chat endpoint in src/worker/index.ts, including request formatting, streaming responses, and error recovery. Cover UI features such as typing indicators, message bubbles, and persistent session handling. Address accessibility, mobile responsiveness, and performance optimization for real-time interactions. Provide guidance on customizing prompts, extending conversation capabilities, and ensuring brand-aligned responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for administrative API endpoints in HabibiStay. Document GET /admin/stats (retrieve platform analytics including total properties, bookings, revenue), GET /admin/settings (fetch platform configuration), PUT /admin/settings (update platform settings), GET /admin/properties (list all properties with filtering), GET /admin/bookings (retrieve all bookings with search capabilities), and PUT /admin/bookings/:id/status (update booking status across all users). For each endpoint, specify HTTP method, URL pattern, request parameters (path, query, body), and response schema. Emphasize strict authentication requirements using JWT with admin role verification. Detail query parameters for pagination, sorting, and filtering in data retrieval endpoints. Include example requests and responses in JSON format, list possible status codes (200, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404, 500), and describe error response structures. Document data validation using Zod schemas for settings updates. Provide sample curl commands for retrieving platform statistics. Reference integration with AdminDashboard.tsx and database schema in migrations. Explain rate limiting and audit logging considerations for sensitive administrative operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reviews Table Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the reviews table defined in migration 3.sql. Detail fields including id, user_id, property_id, booking_id, rating (1-5), comment, is_verified, created_at, and updated_at. Explain referential integrity constraints linking to users, properties, and bookings tables. Map the schema to the Review interface in types.ts and document business rules such as allowing one review per booking and verification logic. Describe query patterns for calculating average ratings per property and retrieving recent verified reviews. Include considerations for preventing review spam and maintaining data quality through application-level validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create RESTful API documentation for all public endpoints exposed by the Hono backend. Document each route's HTTP method, URL pattern, request parameters (path, query, body), and response schema with status codes. Categorize endpoints by resource (properties, bookings, payments, chat, users, admin). Specify authentication requirements (JWT, Google OAuth), required headers, and access permissions. Include detailed examples of request/response payloads in JSON format for successful and error cases. Document data validation rules using Zod schemas, field constraints, and pagination strategies where applicable. Provide sample curl commands for key operations like property search and booking creation.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Journeys
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the user journey mapping in HabibiStay. Define and document the end-to-end flows for four key personas: Guest, Property Owner, Investor, and Admin. For Guests, cover the path from landing on Home, browsing Stays, checking availability, booking, paying, and reviewing. For Owners, detail property listing via PropertyForm, managing bookings in Dashboard, viewing analytics, and receiving payouts. For Investors, describe how the Invest page informs and converts leads through contact submission. For Admins, explain access to AdminDashboard for monitoring metrics, managing content, and configuring platform settings. Use sequence diagrams to show interactions between UI components, API calls, and state changes. Include error handling paths and success feedback mechanisms for each journey.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the administrative endpoints used in the AdminDashboard. Include GET /admin/stats (platform metrics), GET /admin/properties (moderation queue), GET /admin/bookings, PUT /admin/properties/:id (approve/reject), and POST /admin/email-templates. Detail authentication requirements (admin-only JWT), response schemas with aggregated data (total revenue, booking conversion rates), and pagination for large datasets. Explain the analytics calculations for property performance and booking trends. Document the secure email template management system with preview functionality. Provide curl examples for fetching monthly revenue reports and approving a property listing. Include error handling for unauthorized access attempts and rate limiting for admin operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wishlist Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Wishlist entity. Define fields such as id, user_id, property_id, and created_at. Explain the many-to-many relationship between User and Property enabled by this junction table. Document referential integrity with cascade delete on user or property removal. Describe usage patterns including adding/removing properties from wishlist via API and UI components. Map to Wishlist interface in types.ts and detail how the system prevents duplicates. Include indexing on user_id and property_id for fast lookups and describe how wishlist state is synchronized between frontend and backend. Provide examples of wishlist interaction flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wishlist Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Wishlist Functionality. Explain the implementation of adding, removing, and viewing saved properties in the Wishlist page. Document the API endpoints for managing wishlist items and their relationship to user authentication. Describe the frontend interaction patterns, including real-time updates and visual feedback. Include examples of handling concurrent modifications and syncing wishlist state across devices. Address performance considerations for large wishlists and caching strategies. Provide guidance on extending wishlist functionality with sharing capabilities or notifications for price changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wishlist Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the wishlist management API endpoints. Cover POST /api/wishlist (add property to user's wishlist), DELETE /api/wishlist/:propertyId (remove property from wishlist), and GET /api/wishlist (retrieve all wishlist items for current user). Specify authentication via JWT to identify user. Detail request/response schema including property_id and timestamps. Explain how wishlist status is reflected in PropertyCard (heart icon state). Provide JSON examples of wishlist array response with full property details. Include curl commands for adding and removing wishlist items. Address idempotency (duplicate adds should not create multiple entries) and error handling for invalid property IDs. Note performance considerations for fetching wishlist properties with analytics data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wishlist Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Wishlist data model in HabibiStay. Define fields: id, user_id, property_id, and created_at. Document the primary key (id), foreign keys (user_id to User, property_id to Property), and unique constraint on the user_id-property_id combination to prevent duplicates. Explain the one-to-many relationships with User and Property. Include a schema diagram from migration SQL and the corresponding TypeScript interface. Provide a sample wishlist entry. Detail data access patterns in the worker, such as adding/removing properties from wishlist and retrieving a user's saved properties. Discuss performance optimizations like indexing on user_id for fast retrieval and composite indexing for the unique constraint. Address business logic such as ensuring users cannot wishlist their own properties. Explain how wishlist data is used in personalization features and analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components (Navbar & Footer)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Navbar and Footer layout components that provide consistent navigation and branding across all pages in HabibiStay. Explain their implementation as persistent UI elements in App.tsx, using React Router for navigation links and responsive design principles for mobile adaptability. Document the Navbar's features including logo, navigation menu, authentication state display (login/logout), and mobile hamburger menu with Tailwind-based conditional rendering. Cover the Footer's structure with links to legal pages (Privacy, Terms), social media, contact information, and newsletter signup integration. Detail accessibility practices (keyboard navigation, semantic HTML), SEO considerations, and performance impact of shared components. Illustrate how they consume global state (e.g., user authentication) and maintain visual consistency through Tailwind CSS classes and theme configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the user authentication endpoints in HabibiStay, focusing on the Google OAuth integration. Detail GET /auth/google (initiate Google OAuth flow), GET /auth/callback (handle OAuth callback from Google, creates or updates user in database), and GET /auth/me (retrieve current user profile and authentication status). For each endpoint, specify HTTP method, URL pattern, request parameters (query and body), and response schema including user profile data and JWT token. Explain the OAuth 2.0 flow implementation, including state parameter usage for CSRF protection and PKCE if implemented. Document the creation of JWT tokens upon successful authentication and their structure including user ID, role, and expiration. Include example requests and responses in JSON format, list possible status codes (200, 302, 400, 401, 500), and describe error response structures. Explain integration with frontend AuthCallback.tsx and session management. Provide sample curl commands for checking authentication status. Reference environment variables for Google OAuth credentials and security considerations for token storage and transmission.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wishlist Table Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the wishlist table schema introduced in migration 4.sql. Map the schema to relevant types in types.ts and describe how it supports the wishlist functionality in the frontend. Document query patterns for checking if a property is in a user's wishlist and retrieving all wishlist items for a user. Include performance considerations for indexing on user_id and property_id combinations, and describe how wishlist entries are created, removed, and synchronized between client and server.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for all database entities. Detail each table schema from migration files (properties, bookings, payments, users, reviews, wishlist, analytics) including fields, data types, primary/foreign keys, and constraints. Map database tables to TypeScript interfaces in types.ts and explain how Zod validators enforce data integrity. Document entity relationships (e.g., user→properties, property→bookings) and referential integrity rules. Include sample records for each model and describe data lifecycle (creation, updates, soft deletes). Address indexing strategies for query performance and data retention policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Miscellaneous Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document auxiliary endpoints that support marketing and communication features. Cover POST /contact (contact form submission), POST /newsletter (subscription), and POST /invest (investment inquiry). Detail request validation for email formats, rate limiting to prevent spam, and integration with the email service in email.ts. Explain the double-opt-in process for newsletter subscriptions and automated response emails for contact inquiries. Document response schemas with submission IDs and success messages. Include curl examples for submitting a contact form and handling rate limit errors. Address data retention policies for inquiry records and compliance with email marketing regulations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Property Analytics Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the PropertyAnalytics data model used for tracking performance metrics. Although not explicitly defined in migrations, infer structure from usage in worker logic including fields like property_id, view_count, booking_count, revenue_generated, average_rating, and last_updated. Explain how analytics are updated asynchronously via triggers or background jobs after bookings and reviews. Describe read-optimized design for dashboard displays and potential aggregation strategies. Map to relevant analytics interfaces in types.ts and detail how data is accessed by AdminDashboard and owner views. Include performance considerations for real-time vs cached metrics and describe data retention policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Review System. Explain the implementation of submitting and displaying guest reviews through the ReviewModal component. Document the backend validation logic, rating constraints, and database schema for reviews. Describe how reviews are associated with completed bookings and displayed on property pages. Include examples of form validation, star rating implementation, and preventing duplicate reviews. Address moderation workflows, spam detection, and review editing policies. Provide guidance on calculating average ratings and displaying review statistics in analytics dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for the review and rating system endpoints. Document POST /api/reviews (submit a new review with rating 1-5, comment, and verification that user stayed at property), GET /api/reviews/property/:propertyId (fetch all reviews for a property with average rating), and GET /api/reviews/user (get user's submitted reviews). Specify authentication requirements and business logic: users can only review properties they've booked and completed. Detail request schema including property_id, rating, comment, and response schema with user name, date, and verified badge. Include Zod validation for rating range and comment length. Provide JSON examples of review submission and paginated property reviews response. Add curl example for submitting a review. Explain integration with ReviewModal and how average ratings are calculated and displayed on PropertyCard.

wiki_catalogs.name: PropertyAnalytics Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for the PropertyAnalytics entity in HabibiStay. Document all fields: property_id, views_count, bookings_count, revenue_generated, average_rating, and timestamps. Document the primary key (property_id), foreign key to Property, and constraints such as non-negative numeric values. Explain the one-to-one relationship with Property and how analytics are updated via triggers or background jobs. Include a schema diagram from migration files and the TypeScript interface. Provide a sample analytics record with realistic metrics. Describe data access patterns in the worker, such as retrieving analytics for owner dashboards or platform-wide statistics. Discuss performance considerations like atomic updates and aggregation queries. Address data accuracy requirements, update frequency (real-time vs batch), and how analytics inform business decisions. Explain the relationship between raw data (bookings, reviews) and derived metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Marketing Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for marketing and communication endpoints in HabibiStay. Document POST /contact (submit user inquiries from the contact form) and POST /newsletter (subscribe to newsletter with email validation). For each endpoint, specify HTTP method, URL pattern, request parameters (body only), and response schema. Detail request body structure including name, email, message for contact form and email address for newsletter subscription. Explain data validation using Zod schemas to prevent spam and ensure data quality. Document server-side processing including email notifications via src/shared/email.ts and storage in the database. Include example requests and responses in JSON format, list possible status codes (200, 201, 400, 429, 500), and describe error response structures for validation failures and rate limiting. Explain integration with Contact.tsx and any frontend validation. Provide sample curl commands for submitting a contact form. Reference environment configuration for email service and security considerations for handling user contact information and GDPR compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Property Analytics Table Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the property_analytics table defined in migration 5.sql. Detail fields including property_id, view_count, booking_count, revenue_generated, average_rating, and updated_at. Explain how this table supports the analytics dashboard for property owners and admins. Map the schema to relevant analytics types in types.ts and describe the data aggregation mechanisms that update these metrics. Document the relationship with other tables (properties, bookings, reviews) and explain how atomic counters or batch updates maintain accuracy. Include performance benefits of pre-aggregated analytics versus real-time calculation and strategies for handling high-frequency view tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the integrated feature set of HabibiStay. Explain how property management, booking system, payment processing, AI chatbot, and user management work together to deliver the platform's value propositions. Document feature interdependencies, such as how booking availability checks rely on property data and calendar logic, or how payment status updates trigger booking confirmations. Describe the end-to-end user experience across multiple features. Highlight unique differentiators like the AI assistant 'Sara' and Riyadh-specific market focus. Provide use case diagrams showing feature interactions for key user journeys.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Admin Dashboard feature. Explain the implementation of platform-wide statistics, property management tools, and booking oversight in the AdminDashboard component. Document the backend API endpoints that aggregate data from multiple sources and enforce admin-level permissions. Describe the visualization of key metrics like occupancy rates, revenue trends, and user growth. Include examples of filtering, searching, and bulk operations on properties and bookings. Address security considerations for admin access, audit logging, and role-based permissions. Provide guidance on extending the dashboard with custom reports or integration with external analytics tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the administrative API endpoints accessible only to admin users. Cover GET /api/admin/stats (platform analytics: total properties, bookings, revenue, users), GET /api/admin/properties (list all properties with filters), GET /api/admin/bookings (all bookings with search), PUT /api/admin/properties/:id/feature (toggle featured status), and future extensibility points. Specify strict role-based authentication (admin only) using JWT claims. Detail response schema for statistics including time-series data. Include query parameters for date ranges and pagination. Provide JSON example of dashboard statistics response. Add curl command for fetching platform stats (with admin token). Explain security measures: rate limiting, audit logging (future), and protection against mass data exposure. Note integration with AdminDashboard visualizations and how featured properties affect homepage listings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Settings Table Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the admin_settings table introduced in migration 6.sql. Explain the key-value structure with setting_name, setting_value, description, and updated_at fields. Describe how this table enables dynamic configuration of platform features without code deployment. Map the schema to relevant configuration types in types.ts and document common settings such as platform commission rate, booking policies, and feature flags. Explain access control considerations for who can modify settings and audit logging requirements. Include examples of how settings are cached in memory for performance and invalidated on update.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the deployment topology. Describe the Cloudflare Workers and Pages deployment model, including how the frontend (Vite-built assets) and backend (Hono worker) are deployed separately yet integrated. Explain the role of wrangler or similar CLI tools, environment configuration for staging/production, and custom domain setup. Document the D1 database attachment process and KV storage for assets. Cover deployment workflows, rollback strategies, and zero-downtime deployment capabilities. Include monitoring, logging, and error tracking setup considerations for the serverless environment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Newsletter & Marketing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Newsletter & Marketing feature. Explain the implementation of contact forms, newsletter subscriptions, and automated email campaigns. Document the backend handlers for processing inquiries and subscribing users to mailing lists. Describe the integration with the email service utility and template management system. Include examples of double opt-in workflows, unsubscribe mechanisms, and GDPR compliance. Address deliverability best practices, spam prevention, and email template customization. Provide guidance on segmenting user lists and tracking campaign performance metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Marketing Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the marketing and communication endpoints. Cover POST /api/contact (submit contact form with name, email, message), POST /api/newsletter (subscribe email to newsletter), and POST /api/invest (inquiry for property investment). Specify request validation using Zod for email format, message length, and required fields. Detail backend processing: email notifications via configured service (SMTP or provider API) to admin, with user confirmation where applicable. Document response schema (success message or validation errors). Include security measures: rate limiting, spam prevention (honeypot or CAPTCHA placeholder), and GDPR-compliant data handling. Provide JSON examples of contact form submission and success response. Add curl examples for testing contact and newsletter endpoints. Explain integration with Contact, Invest, and potential newsletter signup components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Templates Table Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the email_templates table defined in migration 7.sql. Detail fields including id, template_name, subject, body_html, body_text, placeholders, created_at, and updated_at. Explain how this table supports dynamic email generation for bookings, payments, and newsletters through the email service in email.ts. Map the schema to relevant types in types.ts and describe the placeholder system (e.g., {{user_name}}, {{booking_date}}) that enables personalized messaging. Document versioning considerations for template updates and preview mechanisms for administrators. Include security aspects such as sanitization of HTML content and prevention of template injection attacks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Developer Onboarding Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create a step-by-step getting started guide for new developers. Include prerequisites (Node.js, pnpm, Cloudflare CLI), repository setup, environment variable configuration, and local development setup for both frontend and backend. Provide detailed instructions for running the development server (Vite), connecting to staging database, and testing API endpoints. Document common setup issues and their solutions. Include commands for building, testing, and deploying the application. Add tips for debugging both frontend and worker code, with guidance on using browser dev tools and Cloudflare logs.
